_note: The one-line whyWon: state the one unique thing about each project plainly and directly. What it does, how, then the single fact that makes it different. No setups, contrasts-as-openers, flourishes, cliches, jargon, or em dashes (final-cut.mjs scrubs em dashes into sentence breaks). Applied by scripts/final-cut.mjs by slug.
facetime-macos-ai-agent: You call your own Mac on FaceTime and tell it what to do out loud. It does the task while you're on the call and talks back to you like a person would. No other tool lets you run your computer through a normal video call.
raising-cane: Shepherd is a white cane with a motor in the handle that pushes your hand toward the open path. A blind person just follows it, the way they'd follow a guide dog. Other smart canes only warn you that something is in the way; this one actually steers you around it, and it costs about a twentieth as much.
temp-sqyptg: Identity Matrix is an online world where your character keeps running after you log off. It learns how you talk and act, so your friends can keep chatting with a version of you while you're gone. In every other online game you just disappear when you leave; here you don't.
dial-4rqzc3: The team had nowhere to sleep, so they built AI bots that called real hotels over the phone. The bots talked like people, haggled, and booked them a room for $158 that they actually slept in that night. It ran on live phone calls with real money, not a staged demo.
ross-42pnvi: ROSS is a robot that draws a painting onto your palm so a blind person can feel it. It uses warm and cold touches to stand for different colors, so you can sense where each one is. Nothing else lets a blind person feel a painting's colors as warmth and cold.
orca-4po0nm: You hum or beatbox a tune and tell Orca which instruments you want, and it turns that into a real song you can keep editing. You don't need to know any music software or play any instrument. It builds a finished track from nothing but the sound you make with your voice.
dance-cv: You pick any dance video online and dance along in front of your webcam. Dance CV watches your whole body, scores how close you are, and a voice coach tells you exactly which moves to fix. Unlike games like Just Dance, it works with any video and actually corrects your form.
yes-or-yes: You give YES? or YES! a decision you're stuck on, and three real fish in a tank pick the answer by which side they swim to. The fish make the choice, and the computer carries out whatever they picked. It puts a real decision in your life in the hands of live animals.
screwyouikea: You upload the PDF manual for a piece of IKEA furniture and it becomes a 3D animation. You can spin it around and watch each step happen until the furniture is fully built. It turns the confusing paper instructions everyone struggles with into something you can actually watch.
longshot: You type one sentence describing a game, and 200 AI coding agents work on it at the same time, all night, with no one steering them. By morning they had built a working copy of Minecraft from scratch. Other AI coding tools need you to approve each step; this one runs hundreds of them on their own.
shop-a-sketch: Shop-A-Sketch lets you draw the thing you want to buy instead of describing it in words. It looks at your drawing and finds real products for sale that actually look like it. It searches by what you sketch, not by words you type.
scamshield-87rbxe: ScamShield is a small device that listens along to an older person's phone calls. The moment a caller sounds like a scammer, it warns them out loud and with lights, and texts their family right away. It steps in during the call itself, before any money or information is lost.
snowy-day-3nfbgm: Snowy Day plans the routes snowplows take through a city so every street gets cleared and none get skipped. Each plow works out where to go to avoid overlap and wasted time, and you watch them do it in real time. Most cities still plan plow routes by guesswork; this replaces that.
wordhawk: Wordhawk is a robot that plays the phone game Word Hunt by itself. A camera reads the letters on the screen, the software finds the words, and a robotic finger swipes them out faster than a person can. It doesn't just solve the puzzle, it physically plays the game on a real phone.
diffuji: diffuji is an instant camera that prints art instead of photos. You take a picture and it repaints it in a dreamy style, then prints the result on receipt paper while you wait. You walk away holding a physical print, not just an image on a screen.
freak-cha: freak-cha is a test that proves you're a real person and not an AI. Instead of clicking pictures, you stick your tongue out in different directions on camera. It works because deepfakes still can't fake tongue movements, so it checks for the one thing AI can't do yet.
heartstart: HeartStart is a robot that watches for someone's heart stopping. When it happens, it drives itself across the room to the person and starts doing chest compressions on its own. It keeps CPR going in the minutes before an ambulance arrives, when most bystanders freeze or don't know how.
lifesaver-lnm0tz: LifeSaver lets medical students practice surgery using just a webcam. It tracks their hands, a voice walks them through each step, and they get a score at the end, with no real patient involved. The machines that normally do this cost over $100,000; this does it on a laptop.
kinemo: kinemo is a party game console you play with your body and a webcam, no controllers at all. You jump, duck, and swing your arms to play the games. It gives you a whole console's worth of games using only the camera you already have.
artificial-sandwich-intelligence: This robot learned to make sandwiches just by watching people make them for four hours. It runs on robot arms that cost $200 total, not lab equipment. It shows you can teach a robot a real hands-on task in a weekend for almost no money.
clipper-ph49un: Get Clipped! keeps you focused while studying with your friends. If you quit your study session early, it takes a photo of you and sends it to the group so everyone sees you gave up. Instead of charts and streaks, it uses the fear of getting caught by your friends.
wing-cet3o0: Wing is a safety app for people living with an abuser, hidden inside a working weather app. To anyone who looks, it's just a normal weather app, but the person can quietly reach help and emergency contacts behind it. The disguise means it's safe to keep on the phone even if the abuser checks it.
ted-ai-x8537t: Ted.AI is a teddy bear that can tell how a child is feeling when they hug it. It senses how tight the squeeze is and how fast their heart is beating, then talks back gently. It reads emotions from the hug itself, so the child never has to put how they feel into words.
maestro-n0uqyz: You point a camera at any object, like a broom, and play it like a guitar. Maestro tracks your hands and turns your air-strumming into real notes and chords, which can grow into a full song. It turns everyday objects into instruments, so you don't need to own one.
viniclip: ViniClip edits your video while you're still recording it. If you stumble, pause, or restart a sentence, it cuts those parts out on the spot, so the moment you stop, the video is finished. Normal editing happens after you record; this happens as you talk.
plant-hopper: Plant Hopper is a robotic water-gun turret for your houseplants. It looks around the room, works out which plants are thirsty, then aims and squirts each one the exact amount it needs. It waters each plant individually instead of soaking them all the same.
chessmate-nwygvq: ChessMate is a real chessboard where the pieces slide around by themselves. When you play someone far away, a magnet under the board moves their pieces so both boards always match. You play long-distance chess on a real board instead of on a screen.
readable-ai-braille-printer: BrailleBot is a braille printer built from a cheap 3D printer for under $15. You feed it any document and it punches the whole thing out in braille, and it even describes the pictures in words. Real braille printers cost around $1,500; this does the same job for about a hundredth of the price.
fable-br8e1d: Fable reads your brainwaves with a headband and changes the story as you go. The plot, pictures, and music shift depending on how focused or relaxed you are. Nothing else makes a story react to your brain in real time.
vibe-director: Stu3dio is a little 3D movie studio you walk around in. You talk to a director about the story, a writer about the lines, and an artist about your characters, and it makes the finished short film. Instead of typing into a chat box, you make a movie by walking around and talking to the staff.
signhero: SignHero teaches sign language as a rhythm game. Letters fall down the screen like notes and you score by making the right hand sign in time with the music, while your webcam checks you got it right. It turns practicing sign language into a game you actually want to keep playing.
s-kbd67: This team turned a Nerf gun into a controller for shooter video games. Pulling the trigger shoots in the game, pumping it reloads, and pointing it aims. You play with a real toy gun in your hands instead of a mouse and keyboard.
rehabify-y2f5mu: Rehabify watches you do your physical therapy exercises at home through your webcam. The moment your form slips, it tells you out loud how to fix it, like a therapist in the room. It gives you feedback during the weeks between appointments, when normally no one is checking.
reeljobs: ReelJobs turns job listings into short videos you swipe through like TikTok. Each one is a 20-second clip, and when you see a job you like, one tap fills out the application. It replaces scrolling walls of text and filling out forms with swiping a video feed.
the-unspillable: The Unspillable is a spoon that stays level no matter how badly your hand shakes, so people with tremors can eat without spilling. Two small motors counteract the shaking in real time. The version sold in stores costs over $300; this team built a working one in a weekend for a few dollars.
project-horizon-8iodp2: Project Horizon lets you watch a soccer match through the eyes of any player on the field. Instead of the usual view from above, you see the game the way the striker or the keeper sees it, built from normal TV footage. It puts you inside a player's point of view from video that was never filmed that way.
coach-bob: Coach Bob! is a punching bag you train with through AR glasses. Glowing targets light up on the bag to show where to hit, it measures how hard you punch, and Bob talks trash to keep you going. It turns a boring warm-up into a game so people actually do it.
wateryourdoin: Every question you ask an AI uses real water to cool the computers behind it. WaterYouDoin is a browser add-on that catches questions a normal search could answer and shows how much water you save by skipping them, stacked up as ice cubes. It makes a hidden cost most people don't know about something you can see.
vril: With Vril you describe an object in plain words and it gives you a real 3D model. You can tweak it and send it straight to a 3D printer, with no design software needed. It lets people with no modeling skills design something they can actually print.
digital-physical-darts-wii-darts: This team built a real dart with a motion sensor inside it. You wind up and throw like you actually would, and the dart flies across a dartboard on the screen. They built their own Wii-style motion controller from scratch instead of buying one.
blinkai: BlinkAI lets people who can't move or speak talk by blinking. You blink short and long, like Morse code, and a webcam reads it and speaks full sentences out loud. It gives someone a voice using only their eyes, with no special equipment.
project-lend: Project Lend is a food bank that runs with almost no people. Donors text what they have, a robot arm sorts the food into boxes, and AI arranges the pickups and deliveries. Over one weekend it actually delivered about 100 pounds of food to real shelters.
ghostdiedie: GhostDieDie is a fighting game you play by physically moving in front of your webcam. You and a friend throw real punches and dodge, and you see each other's actual faces on the fighters. Your whole body is the controller, using just the webcam you already have.
lume-zrlypk: Lume is a helper for older people using a computer. You ask it something like how to unmute on Zoom, and it draws a glowing circle right on the button you need to click. It shows you the exact button on your real screen instead of just describing the steps.
replay-wq5isz: Replay records a real room and the people in it using a phone and two small sensors. Afterward you can replay the moment in 3D, pausing, rewinding, and moving the camera anywhere you want. It lets you fly through a real memory like a video game replay.
taqr: EcoTag is portable laser tag where you reload by picking up real litter and scanning it. Every piece of trash you collect gives you more ammo for the next round. It turns cleaning up a park into the game itself.
dose-ebmo9z: Dose is a pill bottle that weighs itself to tell whether someone took their medicine. If the bottle didn't get lighter that day, a dose was missed and the family gets a text. It figures out whether grandma took her pills from weight alone, with nothing for her to do.
whalebeing-27sj0w: WhaleBeing redraws cargo ship routes so they avoid the places whales feed and travel. Ships lose barely any time, and far fewer whales get hit and killed. It shows a deadly problem could be fixed at almost no cost to the ships.
archemy: ARchemy is the game Infinite Craft brought into the real world. Through AR glasses you grab floating objects with your bare hands and smash them together to invent new ones, so a duck and a car make a quackmobile. You play it with your actual hands in the air instead of clicking on a screen.
resqme-jq52al: ResQme is a set of cheap pocket radios that keep working when cell towers and wifi go down. Each one passes your text and location to the next device until it reaches rescuers. It keeps people connected in a disaster exactly when every normal network has failed.
